channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: California couple on vacation fatally shot in Mexico, officials say
item.description: An American couple from Northern California was shot and killed by gunmen in the western Mexican state of Michoacán, according to officials.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge sentences Zephen Xaver, man who executed 5 women at Florida bank, to death
item.description: Zephen Xaver pleaded guilty to five counts of murder for killing Ana Piñon Williams, Debra Cook, Marisol Lopez, Cynthia Watson and Jessica Montague at a SunTrust Bank in Sebring, Florida in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawmakers signal movement toward government funding deal
item.description: Top Republicans are signaling progress in government funding talks as leaders look to clinch a deal ahead of a looming Friday deadline. House Appropriations Chair Tom Cole (R-Okla.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Ingrid Lewis-Martin, chief adviser to NYC Mayor Eric Adams, resigns and expects to be indicted
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ingrid Lewis-Martin, a former top aide to New York Mayor Eric Adams, expects to be indicted on criminal charges related to alleged improper gifts, her lawyer said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: France rushes aid to Mayotte after Cyclone Chido leaves hundreds feared dead
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — France used ships and military aircraft to rush rescuers and supplies to Mayotte on Monday after the tiny French island territory off Africa was battered by its worst cyclone in nearly a century. Authorities fear hundreds and possibly thousands of people have died.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, THOMAS ADAMSON and RAINAT ALILOIFFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Palestinian security forces launch a rare crackdown on militants in the West Bank
item.description: JENIN, West Bank (AP) — Palestinian security forces have launched a rare crackdown against local militant groups in the northern West Bank, sending in armored cars and engaging in fierce gunbattles that have killed at least two people in the volatile area.
item.dc:creator: MAJDI MOHAMMED and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Zakir Hussain, one of India's most accomplished classical musicians, dies at 73
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Zakir Hussain, one of India's most accomplished classical musicians who defied genres and introduced tabla to global audiences, died on Sunday. He was 73.
item.dc:creator: SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump, Softbank CEO announce $100B SoftBank investment in the US
item.description: resident-elect Donald Trump will join SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son at his Florida home on Monday to announce that the Japanese company is planning to invest $100 billion in U.S. projects over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, Fox 5

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the U.S. Here's what we know
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey and across the eastern U.S., sparking speculation and concern over where they came from and why. New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy and U.S. Sen.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man sentenced for helping pregnant Chinese women travel to give birth in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A California man was sentenced Monday to nearly 3 1/2 years in prison for running an extensive business that helped pregnant Chinese women travel to the United States to deliver babies who would automatically have American citizenship. U.S.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Latest: 3 dead in a shooting at a private Christian school in Madison, Wisconsin
item.description: Police say a student opened fire at a private Christian school Monday morning in Madison, Wisconsin, killing two people in the final week before Christmas break.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How old are Saturn's rings? Study suggests they could be 4.5 billion years old just like the planet
item.description: New research suggests that Saturn's rings may be older than they look — possibly as old as the planet. Instead of being a youthful 400 million years old as commonly thought, the icy, shimmering rings could be 4.5 billion years old just like Saturn.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN AP Aerospace Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Teacher and student killed, 6 injured in Wisconsin school shooting
item.description: A teacher and a student were killed in a shooting at the Abundant Life Christian School in Madison, Wisconsin, police said Monday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Devin Willems, KREX 5

item.title: 3 dead, others injured in a shooting at a private Christian school in Wisconsin
item.description: A teenage student opened fire at a private Christian school Monday morning in Wisconsin, killing a teacher and another student in the final week before Christmas break.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Missouri's death row had nearly 100 inmates in the 1990s. Now, it has eight
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — Missouri 's status as one of the most active death penalty states is about to change for one simple reason: The state is running out of inmates to execute. The lethal injection of Christopher Collings on Dec.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Drone sightings lead to airspace shutdown at Ohio military base, arrests near Boston airport
item.description: More suspected drone sightings in the eastern United States have led to a temporary airspace shutdown around an Air Force base in Ohio and arrests near Boston’s Logan International Airport.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins And Bianca Vázquez Toness, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: JonBenet Ramsey's dad suggests daughter’s killer motivated by money in resurfaced interview
item.description: The killer of JonBenet Ramsey, who was murdered in her Colorado home in 1996, may have been motivated by money, her father told Barbara Walters in a resurfaced interview.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jill Jacobson, actress who appeared on 'Star Trek' and 'Falcon Crest,' dies at 70
item.description: "We are incredibly sad to say goodbye to our beautiful, soulful, hysterically funny, elegantly raunchy client, Jill Jacobson," her manager Ben Padua told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McConnell urges Trump to reject isolationism
item.description: Outgoing Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) is calling on President-elect Trump to reject isolationists in the Republican Party who will argue for the United States to focus on China at the expense of Ukraine and other areas of the world.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump girds for battle with Democrats, Supreme Court over birthright citizenship
item.description: President-elect Trump, Democratic lawmakers and the Supreme Court are on a collision course over restricting birthright citizenship, which Trump has identified as a top priority for his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Report documents family separations during 1st Trump term
item.description: A report examining years of family separation policies enacted under the Trump administration is being released Monday by several migrant advocacy nonprofit organizations to remind the public and lawmakers of what transpired, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego man dies after being mauled by his own dogs: police
item.description: A man was mauled by his three dogs at a neighborhood park Thursday afternoon, according to the San Diego Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Tony Shin, KRON4

item.title: Two states sue Glock over add-on allowing machine gun-like firing
item.description: Officials in New Jersey and Minnesota filed a lawsuit against gun manufacturer Glock Thursday, demanding the company suspend its sale of weapons that can be easily converted with a switch to allow over 1,000 rounds of rapid fire.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: California DMV apologizes for Cybertruck's license plate but family cites misinterpretation
item.description: On Thursday, the license plate in question, which was on a Tesla Cybertruck, was spotlighted in a post to X by StopAntisemitism, a group “dedicated to fighting anti-Semitism.” The family, however, argues it is being misread.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, WGN 9

item.title: Krispy Kreme offering green doughnuts for 2 days only
item.description: The chain also has a limited time “Merry Grinchmas Doughnut Collection” to celebrate the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court will take up a challenge related to California's tough vehicle emissions standards
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday said it would take up a business-backed appeal that could make it easier to challenge federal regulations, acting in a dispute related to California's nation-leading standards for vehicle emissions.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Taylor Swift visits patients at Children's Mercy Hospital in Kansas City
item.description: Fresh off her final Era's Tour stop Sunday in Canada, Taylor Swift made a surprise visit at Children's Mercy Hospital.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court will hear Catholic charitable group's plea to be free from Wisconsin unemployment tax
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday said it would take up a new religious rights case over whether a Catholic charitable organization must pay Wisconsin's employment tax.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Prosecutors will seek the death penalty for 2 Venezuelan men accused of killing Texas girl
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Prosecutors in Texas announced Friday that they will seek the death penalty against two Venezuelan men who are accused of killing a 12-year-old Houston girl after they had entered the U.S. illegally.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey governor wants more federal resources for probe into drone sightings
item.description: TOMS RIVER, N.J. (AP) — Gov. Phil Murphy has asked the Biden administration to put more resources into an investigation of mysterious drone sightings that have been reported in New Jersey and nearby states.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A man has pleaded guilty to the death of a 5-year-old girl living in their Kansas homeless camp
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A man who lived in a makeshift camp with a homeless 5-year-old Kansas girl and her father pleaded guilty Friday to first-degree murder and rape in connection with the child’s death.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: OpenAI's Altman will donate $1 million to Trump's inaugural fund
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is planning to make a $1 million personal donation to President-Elect Donald Trump's inauguration fund, joining a number of tech companies and executives who are working to improve their relationships with the incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Conan O'Brien's parents die within days of each other
item.description: Dr. Thomas O’Brien and Ruth R. O’Brien, the parents of late-night icon Conan O’Brien, both passed away within days of each other at their home in Brookline, Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: TikTok's 'slotmachinequeen' charged with nearly $1 million charity fraud
item.description: The Limestone County Sheriff's Office said Downs solicited donations for a charity to aid domestic violence victims and homeless individuals.
item.dc:creator: Josiah Elmore, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Duke lacrosse players' rape accuser admits she lied
item.description: A woman who accused three former Duke University men’s lacrosse players of rape nearly two decades ago admitted she lied about the allegations and asked them for forgiveness.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Kylie Kelce dethrones Joe Rogan on podcast charts as her debut episode hits No. 1
item.description: Kylie Kelce, wife of former NFL player turned media personality Jason Kelce, beat out Joe Rogan for the top podcast spot on both Apple and Spotify this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9
